mechanism_narrative: A sustained decline in the federal funds rate signals an easing monetary cycle that compresses discount rates applied to equity cash flows, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in easing periods are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: Rising consumer price inflation (CPIAUCSL) above 245 signals central bank tightening pressure, leading to negative S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days as investors price in higher discount rates.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 5d returns in > 60% of triggered instances despite CPIAUCSL > 245, or Rank IC < 0.03 over any 3-year window.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous declines in FEDFUNDS and CPIAUCSL indicate accommodative policy and disinflation, creating a positive feedback loop for equity valuations through lower real rates and improved earnings visibility over 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Interaction effect reverses direction (negative 20d returns) in 40%+ of co-moving FEDFUNDS/CPIAUCSL declines, or pairwise correlation < 0.2 in 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: A 0.3% monthly decline in UNRATE signals tightening labor markets and potential wage inflation, leading to negative S&P 500 returns over 60 trading days as earnings expectations adjust downward.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 60d returns in > 50% of triggered instances, or wage growth (CPIAUCSL) fails to accelerate within 3 months of UNRATE decline.

mechanism_narrative: Accelerating industrial production (INDPRO) above 105 signals robust manufacturing activity, generating positive S&P 500 returns over 20 trading days through improved corporate earnings visibility.
failure_condition: SPX produces negative 20d returns in > 40% of triggered INDPRO > 105 instances, or INDPRO fails to correlate with SPX returns (Pearson < 0.15) in 3-year window.

mechanism_narrative: A 1.5% quarterly increase in GDP (GDPC1) indicates strong economic growth, driving positive S&P 500 returns over 60 trading days through elevated corporate profit expectations.
failure_condition: SPX produces negative 60d returns in > 30% of triggered GDPC1 growth periods, or GDP growth fails to predict SPX returns (Rank IC < 0.05) in 5-year window.

mechanism_narrative: Strong nonfarm payrolls (PAYEMS) above 150k signal sustained labor market strength, generating positive S&P 500 returns over 5 trading days through improved consumer spending prospects.
failure_condition: SPX produces negative 5d returns in > 50% of triggered PAYEMS > 150k instances, or payrolls fail to correlate with SPX returns (Pearson < 0.1) in 3-year window.

mechanism_narrative: Declining industrial production (INDPRO) below 104 signals manufacturing contraction, leading to negative S&P 500 returns over 20 trading days through reduced corporate earnings and sector-specific risks.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 20d returns in > 40% of triggered INDPRO < 104 instances, or INDPRO fails to predict SPX returns (Rank IC < 0.03) in 5-year window.
